brief_document.title: End-to-end record-then-replay round-trip
brief_document.purpose: Prove the whole durability loop closes: events recorded through the Recorder, when replayed from the same store, reproduce the same Resolutions in order, land on the correct resume point, and reproduce the same workflow.now timestamps and workflow.random draws. This is the integration proof that recording and replay are two halves of one mechanism and that determinism holds across independent replays.
brief_document.task: Add an integration test (in tests/replay_behaviour.rs or a dedicated tests/round_trip.rs) that exercises the full record-then-replay loop over InMemoryStore. First, record a workflow's lifecycle (start, several activities, a timer, a signal, completion) via the Recorder using engine-supplied recorded timestamps. Then construct a fresh replay from the same store and assert the replayed Resolutions match the recorded outcomes in order and the resume point is terminal. Separately, assert determinism: workflow.now during replay equals the recorded event timestamps in order, and two independent replays of the same run produce identical random draws. No new production logic beyond what AD-001..009 deliver — this brief is the integration assertion layer.

requirements.title: Record-then-replay Resolution round-trip
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an integration test that records a workflow's full event history via the Recorder and then replays from the same store, asserting the replayed Resolutions equal the recorded outcomes in order and the resume point is terminal.
acceptance: The test records WorkflowStarted, then ActivityScheduled/Completed for two activities, a TimerStarted/TimerFired, a SignalReceived, and WorkflowCompleted, all via the Recorder into InMemoryStore with monotonically increasing engine-supplied recorded timestamps.

It then builds a replay from read_history and drives the matching command stream, asserting each Resolution equals the recorded outcome (activity result Payloads, timer fired, signal payload) in order.
It asserts the replay reaches terminal with no ResumeLive (the history is complete).
The test is a real runtime test (not #[ignore], not env-gated) and passes under cargo test.

requirements.title: Determinism round-trip: now and random
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide an integration test asserting that workflow.now values during replay equal the recorded event timestamps in order, and that workflow.random draws are identical across two independent replays of the same run.
acceptance: During the replay of R1's history, the test reads DeterminismContext::now() as each event is consumed and asserts it equals that event's recorded timestamp, in order.
The test runs two independent replays of the same WorkflowId+RunId, drawing the same number of random values from each, and asserts the two draw sequences are byte-for-byte identical.
A negative assertion confirms a replay of a different RunId yields a different random sequence.
The test is a real runtime test and passes under cargo test.

boundaries: SHALL NOT add new production behaviour — it asserts the integrated behaviour of AD-001 through AD-009.

SHALL NOT depend on AT, AF, or a real beamr process — it drives the resolution loop directly with a test command stream and a mock/absent LiveExecutor (the history is complete, so no live calls occur).
SHALL NOT use a durable backend — InMemoryStore only (durable round-trip across process restart is exercised by AS/AX).
verification: cargo test -p aion passes, including the round-trip integration tests.
The now-equals-recorded-timestamp and random-identical-across-replays assertions pass (CO8, CO9).
No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.

checklist.text: An integration test records a workflow's events via the Recorder, then replays from the same store and asserts the replayed Resolutions match the recorded outcomes in order and the resume point is correct.
An integration test asserts workflow.now values during replay equal the recorded event timestamps in order, and that random draws match across two independent replays of the same run.

stories.text: As the engine, I want to replay a workflow from its recorded history and have every activity, timer, signal, and child call return its recorded outcome so that I reconstruct the exact prior state without repeating side effects.

As a workflow author, I want workflow.now to return the recorded event time and workflow.random to return a workflow-seeded value so that my workflow replays to exactly the same state every time without me thinking about it.
As a workflow author, I want replay to be invisible so that I write a normal deterministic function and never code differently for the replay case.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny across the whole module set.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] bypasses; tests use InMemoryStore in-process, never env-gated skips.
mod.rs holds only pub mod declarations and re-exports; logic lives in named files.
500-line file limit (excluding tests/comments/whitespace); split the Resolver and replay driver before they exceed it.
aion::durability depends only on aion-core and aion-store (plus an RNG crate and chrono); no AT, AF, networking, or direct beamr — runtime concerns are reached only through the LiveExecutor trait.

Single-writer discipline is structural: only a Recorder appends to a workflow's history, exactly one Recorder per active workflow; no other path calls EventStore::append for a workflow with a live Recorder.

Every append states an expected_seq from the Recorder's tracked head; the head is never guessed or read from outside the Recorder, and a SequenceConflict is surfaced as a hard error (double-writer bug), not retried by re-reading.

workflow.now derives only from the recorded event timestamp in the DeterminismContext; no code path here calls the wall clock to produce a workflow-visible value. The recovery wall time for expired_timers is explicitly not workflow-visible.
workflow.random derives only from the seed (WorkflowId + RunId); no entropy source is consulted; same run -> identical random sequence.
A non-determinism violation is always raised as a typed NonDeterminismError at the point of mismatch; never swallowed, never guessed past, never allowed to produce a Resolution.
Replay never invokes a live side effect: while the cursor can satisfy a command the LiveExecutor is not called; it is called only after ResumeLive.
Determinism-violation detection has dedicated test coverage: a recorded history that deliberately diverges from the replayed command stream asserts the typed error.

resolved_context.intention: Make Aion durable. Everything else in the engine is mechanics a non-durable orchestrator could also have; this layer is what lets a workflow crash on step nine of ten and resume exactly where it was, sleep for three months and resume in the same logical state, and run as if it ran once even after five replays. It does three things: it records (every externally-observable engine action becomes an Event appended to the EventStore under a single-writer-per-workflow discipline with an expected-sequence guard); it replays (re-runs the workflow function from the start, and for every world-touching call consults recorded history and returns the recorded outcome instead of acting again); and it enforces determinism (supplies the recorded timestamp for workflow.now, a workflow-seeded value for workflow.random, and detects when a replay diverges from recorded history — failing loudly rather than corrupting state silently). When done, the engine (AE) spawns a workflow process and hands it a resolver that transparently answers every world-touching call from history during replay and falls through to live execution at the resume point; recovery becomes list_active, replay each, resume. The workflow author writes a plain deterministic function and never knows whether a call executed for real or returned a cached result.

provenance.quote: Make Aion durable. Everything else in the engine is mechanics a non-durable orchestrator could also have; this layer is what lets a workflow crash on step nine of ten and resume exactly where it was,
